item.title: California governor, LA mayoral races still unsettled as vote count continues
item.description: Outcomes in key races for governor and LA mayor remain unsettled as mayoral hopefuls Spencer Pratt and Nithya Raman, as well as gubernatorial candidates Xavier Becerra, Steve Hilton and Tom Steyer await their electoral fates.

item.title: California primary results for key races including governor, LA mayor
item.description: The races for governor and Los Angeles mayor are among the most closely watched contests yet to be decided in California's primary elections.

item.title: Dow soars nearly 900 points as Wall Street bets on Iran peace deal
item.description: Despite flare-ups in Middle East violence, investors remain optimistic that the U.S. and Iran will soon end the war.

item.title: Trump announces $700 million investment in coal plants, California export terminal
item.description: President Trump on Thursday announced plans for his administration to commit $700 million in funding for coal plants and a new export terminal. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has details on the announcement and Mike O'Boyle, senior director for policy and strategy at Energy Innovation, joins with analysis.

item.title: Iran says "no tangible progress" in talks; Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce
item.description: Iran says there's been no progress in talks with the U.S. after tit-for-tat strikes, as Hezbollah rejects new Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

item.title: Coast Guard seizes dinghy in Bahamas that Lynette Hooker allegedly "bounced off" of
item.description: The eight-foot dinghy that Brian Hooker says he and his wife, Lynette Hooker,​ were aboard when she disappeared in early April was seized by U.S. Coast Guard investigators.

item.title: Trump says Bill Pulte won't be director of national intelligence permanently
item.description: Asked if Bill Pulte has the national security experience for the job, President Trump said he does because he's "smart."

item.title: Latest on Senate "vote-a-rama" on ICE funding, move by Democrats to block "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: The Senate is in the middle of a vote-a-rama, where they can offer an unlimited number of amendments to a piece of legislation and require the chamber to cast vote after vote. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.

item.title: From the archives: "Persepolis" author-filmmaker Marjane Satrapi
item.description: Iranian-French cartoonist Marjane Satrapi, who won acclaim for her graphic novel "Persepolis" about growing up in Tehran during the Islamic Revolution, died on June 4, 2026 at age 56. In this April 20, 2008 "Sunday Morning" interview, Satrapi talked with correspondent Serena Altschul about telling her family's story, and about adapting her black-and-white book into an Oscar-nominated animated film voiced by Catherine Deneuve and Chiara Mastroianni.

item.title: House blocks Rep. Rashida Tlaib's war powers resolution on Lebanon
item.description: Democratic leaders came out against the resolution before the vote, saying they favor another one introduced by Tlaib on Wednesday.

item.title: Bipartisan Senate duo urges court to maintain block on DOJ fund
item.description: Sens. Bill Cassidy, a Louisiana Republican, and Cory Booker, a New Jersey Democrat, said the anti-weaponization fund violates multiple constitutional provisions.

item.title: Trump announces $700 million investment in coal industry
item.description: The money will fund new and existing coal plants, as well as an export terminal in Oakland, California.

item.title: Colo. Court of Appeals reverses former paramedics' convictions in McClain death
item.description: The Colorado Court of Appeals reversed the criminally negligent homicide convictions for the former paramedics in the death of Elijah McClain.

item.title: Behind the history of "This Land is Your Land" and how it has inspired Americans
item.description: In the series "USA to Z," which celebrates 250 years of American history and culture, "CBS Mornings" shares the backstory and meaning behind the popular song "This Land is Your Land."

item.title: 1 dead, 3 hurt in high school graduation shooting in Northern California
item.description: One person died and three others, including a child, were injured in a shooting Wednesday night at a high school graduation in Northern California, officials said.

item.title: Meta is selling $3.99 monthly subscriptions for Instagram Plus
item.description: The new paid tier adds features like longer stories and deeper metrics as Meta looks to diversify revenue beyond advertising.

item.title: The U.S. is minting more millionaires than ever. Here's why.
item.description: A stock market boom is elevating more Americans into the ranks of the nation's millionaires, a new study finds.

item.title: What are Freedom 250 and America250? Behind the 2 groups planning celebrations
item.description: A musical concert series has become a point of political contention, with performers dropping out of the series.

item.title: Trump's name must come off the Kennedy Center by June 12
item.description: There's been no word yet on if the Kennedy Center plans to remain open after July 5. It was to be closed for two years for extensive repairs beginning this summer.

item.title: Senate holds "vote-a-rama" on ICE funding, rejects move to ban DOJ fund
item.description: The Senate will soon hold what's expected to be a marathon vote series as Republicans seek to fund immigration agencies under the Department of Homeland Security without help from Democrats.

item.title: What borrowers need to consider before the July federal student loan changes
item.description: Borrowers have a short window to prepare for numerous upcoming student loan changes. Here's where to start.

item.title: Pratt, Raman locked in battle for Los Angeles mayoral runoff spot
item.description: CBS News projects that incumbent Mayor Karen Bass will advance to the November election, while her opponents, Councilmember Nithya Raman and political newcomer Spencer Pratt, compete for the final spot.

item.title: Alleged $30M fraud ring involving children's health services busted, officials say
item.description: Federal authorities have busted what they say is a $30 million fraud conspiracy involving billing for children's behavioral health services that were never provided, officials announced.

item.title: Luxury cars seized amid alleged $30 million fraud ring involving children's health services
item.description: Federal law enforcement officials on Thursday announced that two Ohio state employees and two co-conspirators were indicted in connection with an alleged $30 million Medicaid billing fraud scheme involving children's behavioral health services that were never rendered. As part of the investigation, 14 vehicles were seized, including a Maserati, a Mercedes, a Bentley and a McLaren, according to a source. CBS News senior Justice Department Sarah Lynch has more.

item.title: Social Security could be cut by $500 monthly in 2032, a new report claims. Here's how to boost your savings before that happens.
item.description: Social Security could reportedly be cut by $500 per month in 2032. Here's how to increase your savings before then.

item.title: Sun erupts with 3 solar flares, raising chances of northern lights for U.S.
item.description: Three solar flares burst from the sun this week, raising the chances of seeing the northern lights for people across the United States.

item.title: Guide missing for a week on Mount Everest found alive: "Miracle"
item.description: A Sherpa guide was found crawling to base camp on Mount Everest a week after he went missing.

item.title: California governor's race narrows as Hilton, Becerra take early lead
item.description: Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer have emerged as the leading contenders to advance to November's general election, according to early counts.

item.title: Will CD account interest rates rise this summer? Experts weigh in
item.description: You can still earn plenty of interest with a CD account this summer, whether or not CD rates increase.

item.title: Late California ballots could reshape primary results in closely watched governor's race
item.description: California's governor's race is tightening as many Democratic voters appear to be waiting until the final hours to cast their ballots.
